lang.label: Language
login.title: Sign in
totp.title: Two-factor verification
listing.subtitle: Services
listing.title: Available services
listing.empty: No services available for your profile.
profile.subtitle: Profile
profile.title: My profile
profile.admin_all: As an administrator you can access all services.
profile.2fa_hint: Regenerate the TOTP secret if you changed device or lost the app. You will scan a new QR; your next sign-in uses the new code.
profile.2fa_confirm: Regenerate 2FA? The old code will stop working.
qr.scan: Scan the QR with your authenticator app (or enter the key manually). Your next sign-in uses this code.
blocked.title: Restricted area
blocked.not_admin: This account is not an administrator.
blocked.need_admin: The administration area requires an account with the admin flag.
admin.title: Administration
admin.subtitle: Administration
admin.sec.title: Security — IPs allowed to the admin area
admin.sec.hint: Only these IPs/networks can reach /admin. Current source:
admin.sec.field: Allowed IPs/CIDRs (space or comma; single IP = /32)
admin.sec.warn: The new list must include your IP, otherwise you would lock yourself out. Empty the field to revert to config.json/ADMIN_CIDR.
admin.svc.hint: Services from config.json are read-only (infrastructure). Those added here live in services.json.
admin.col.name: name
admin.rule.help: public: open to everyone. authenticated: users with a valid session (2FA). whitelist: only explicitly authorized users (implies authentication); administrators always have access (override).
admin.f.name: name
admin.links.hint: Tiles in the dashboard, not proxied.
admin.dk.hint: Running containers with published ports. «Add» creates a vhost <name>.localhost → host.docker.internal:<port>.
admin.dk.disabled: Docker discovery is off (DOCKER_PROXY not set).
admin.dk.portmap_hint: Services already routed to a host port (host.docker.internal).
admin.dk.hostports_hint: Find ports listening on the host (e.g. PuTTY/SSH tunnels to remote servers) to expose as a vhost.
admin.usr.admin_all_note: This user is an administrator: they access all services, the whitelist does not apply.
admin.mon.custom_hint: HTTP probes independent of the proxied backends (they also appear in the Status table).
admin.edit.title: Edit service
admin.edit.ipallow: IP allow-list (CIDR, optional — empty = no limit)
admin.qr.title: 2FA for
admin.qr.hint: Have the user scan the QR with their authenticator app, or share the key.
admin.hs.subtitle: Host ports
admin.hs.hint: Ports reachable via host.docker.internal (e.g. PuTTY/SSH tunnels, host services). Tick the ports and add them in bulk; empty name → host-<port>.
admin.hs.none: no open port in the range
setup.cred.title: Initial setup
setup.totp.title: Enable 2FA
setup.done.title: Setup complete
